title: Legacy history table rebuild runs outside a transaction → permanent audit-log loss on mid-migration crash

findingDetails.evidence: history.init runs ALTER TABLE RENAME -> CREATE -> INSERT...SELECT -> DROP as four separate auto-committing database.exec calls (L103-133). Its caller initHistory(database) is at vector-index-schema.ts:2024, AFTER run_all_migrations() (transaction L1964) has already committed and returned at L1974-1978 — so the rebuild has no enclosing transaction. The re-run guard at history.ts:101 only fires when the table SQL still contains 'CHECK(actor IN' or 'FOREIGN KEY'; the rebuilt table has neither.
findingDetails.why: A crash/SIGKILL between RENAME and INSERT (plausible under this epic's daemon re-election/restart) leaves an empty new memory_history plus orphaned memory_history_old. On next init the guard is false, so the INSERT never re-runs and the entire history log is stranded permanently — silent data loss of the change-audit trail.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wrap the rebuild block (history.ts:103-133) in database.transaction(() => { ... })() so the RENAME/CREATE/INSERT/DROP either all commit or all roll back; SQLite DDL is transaction-safe for these statements.

title: specFolderCache never invalidated → stale spec_folder attribution on history records

findingDetails.evidence: resolveSpecFolder returns the cached value whenever defined (L241-243); the cache is cleared only on init() (L155) and refreshed only when an explicit override is passed (L236-238). reconsolidation.ts:393 records an UPDATE event with no spec_folder argument, falling back to cache/index.
findingDetails.why: If a memory's spec_folder changes after its first resolution, later recordHistory calls without an override attach the OLD folder to new rows, mis-attributing them in getHistoryStats and lineage views.
findingDetails.recommendation: Invalidate or overwrite the cache entry when memory_index.spec_folder is updated for that id, or have UPDATE callers pass the current spec_folder explicitly.

title: reset() and overflow-flush discard accumulated counts when flush fails

findingDetails.evidence: reset() (L251-255) and the MAX_ACCUMULATOR_SIZE overflow path (L93-97) call flushAccessCounts best-effort, ignore its boolean return, then accumulators.clear() unconditionally. trackAccess (L108-110) by contrast re-stores newValue when flush returns false.
findingDetails.why: A transient flush failure (e.g. SQLITE_BUSY) silently drops the accumulated access counts during periodic/overflow flush, inconsistent with trackAccess's retry-preserving behavior. Low blast radius (popularity scoring only) but an avoidable count loss.
findingDetails.recommendation: Track per-id flush success and only delete/clear the entries whose flush returned true; retain the rest for the next flush cycle.
